'''Brett Northcutt''' is a digimatte artist for [[Industrial Light & Magic]] that created the landscapes of [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], and the [[Emperor Palpatine Surgical Reconstruction Center]]. He later created concept art for [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']].

His resume spans more than 30 other films, including [[Wikipedia:X-Men|''X-Men'']], [[Wikipedia:Hulk|''Hulk'']], [[Wikipedia:The Truman Show|''The Truman Show'']], [[Wikipedia:Jarhead|''Jarhead'']], and most recently [[Wikipedia:Pirates of the Caribbean 2: Dead Man's Chest|''Pirates of the Caribbean 2: Dead Man's Chest'']].

==Biography==
Northcutt attended the Savannah College of Art and Design and the Art Institute of Chicago, before moving to California to realize his dream of digital art. He studied under matte artist [[Chris Evans]] who worked on [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. Speaking of Evans, Northcutt has said that he "could not have asked for a more skilled or more humble master". He then joined ILM in [[2001]], but not to work on [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], but ''Gangs of New York''. He finally had his chance with [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].
